title: Antibacterial oxygenated ergostane-type steroids produced by the marine sponge-derived fungus Aspergillus sp.
author_string: Wen, Hui-Min, Zhang, Ya-Wen, Feng, Fang-Jian, Huang, Guo-Bao, Lv, Yu-Han et al.
journal_full: Journal of Asian natural products research
abstract: Two oxygenated ergostane-type steroids including one new compound, 3β-hydroxy-5α,6β-methoxyergosta-7,22-dien-15-one (1) along with a known analogue ergosta-6,22-dien-3β,5α,8α-triol (2) were isolated from the crude extracts of the marine sponge-derived fungus Aspergillus sp. Their structures were elucidated on the basis of combined NMR and MS spectroscopic methods. Compound 1 was a marine ergostane-type steroid with two methoxy groups at C-5 and C-6, respectively. These oxygenated ergostane-type steroids were evaluated for their antibacterial activities against human or aquatic pathogens. Among them, compound 1 exhibited antibacterial activity against Staphylococcus aureus.
